context.description: German philosophical prose explains an exercise in expanding awareness from point attention to broader field awareness. It describes inner inertia, partial forgetting, and the difficulty of sustaining awareness as the next step toward dual-track awareness.
normalization_notes: Translated visible German prose into English summary and keywords.
Classified as nonfiction body prose with theoretical discussion based on continuous reflective paragraphs.

rows.paragraph_text: Awareness can move rapidly between points in the visual field, faster than the eyes can follow. The text describes expanding from point attention to awareness of the whole field, then extending that awareness into the inner world.

It emphasizes inner inertia as a force that pulls awareness back into point attention and makes sustaining the exercise effortful. The passage also notes partial forgetting and the resistance that appears when trying to continue.
The next step is presented as dual-track awareness, with a warning that practice may provoke frustration and a return to eye movement or abandonment of the exercise.

content.free_text: book_page book_body_content theoretical_discussion German philosophical book page on awareness training, point attention, inner inertia, partial forgetting, and dual-track awareness.
